# ai-apps samples

> Runnable samples and reference scenarios for building AI-native applications
> on Azure Container Apps. Six pillars: sandboxes, containerapps, connectors,
> triggers, ai-gateway, and ai-apps (cross-pillar). Every sample is
> self-contained — `cd` into a folder, install its requirements.txt, run.

See [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) for idioms and conventions when generating code
that uses these samples.

## Setup (run once per pillar)

- [Sandboxes setup](sandboxes/setup/README.md): Provisions resource group +
  sandbox group + RBAC. Run `setup/python/setup.py` (Python SDK flow) **or**
  `setup/cli/setup.sh` (CLI flow, no Python). Writes `samples/.env`.

## Sandboxes — guides (one capability per script)

- [00-sandbox-groups](sandboxes/guides/00-sandbox-groups/README.md):
  Create a sandbox group (ARM), assign the data-owner role, run a sandbox, delete the group. Python + CLI.
- [01-sandboxes](sandboxes/guides/01-sandboxes/README.md):
  Create a sandbox, run a shell command, delete it. Basic + advanced (sync)
  plus parallel boot of 3 via `azure.containerapps.sandbox.aio` +
  `asyncio.gather` (Python). CLI variant adds `aca sandbox apply --file
  sandbox.yaml` so the same advanced create is also expressed as a
  checked-in spec file.
- [02-snapshots](sandboxes/guides/02-snapshots/README.md):
  `create_snapshot`, boot a new sandbox from the snapshot, verify state preserved. Python + CLI.
- [03-disks](sandboxes/guides/03-disks/README.md):
  Build a disk image from a public OCI image **and** commit a running
  sandbox to a disk image — both flows in one guide. Python + CLI.
- [04-volumes](sandboxes/guides/04-volumes/README.md):
  Shared AzureBlob volumes — producer writes, consumer reads, via `add_volume_mount`. Python + CLI.
- [05-lifecycle](sandboxes/guides/05-lifecycle/README.md):
  `stop` / `resume` / `wait_for_running` / `ensure_running` + `AutoSuspendPolicy` + `AutoDeletePolicy`. Python + CLI.
- [06-ports](sandboxes/guides/06-ports/README.md):
  `add_port(anonymous=True)`, fetch the public URL, `remove_port`. Python + CLI.
- [07-files](sandboxes/guides/07-files/README.md):
  `write_file` / `read_file` / `stat_file` / `list_files` / `mkdir` / `delete_file`. Python + CLI.
- [08-egress](sandboxes/guides/08-egress/README.md):
  `set_egress_default("Deny")` + `add_egress_host_rule` to lock down outbound network. Python + CLI.
- [09-secrets](sandboxes/guides/09-secrets/README.md):
  Group-scoped secrets — `upsert_secret` / `list_secrets` / `peek_secret` / `delete_secret`. Python + CLI.
- [10-identity](sandboxes/guides/10-identity/README.md):
  Identity on a sandbox group — System-assigned / User-assigned managed identity today; foundation for agent inception. Python + CLI.
- [11-labels](sandboxes/guides/11-labels/README.md):
  `labels=` on `begin_create_sandbox` + `list_sandboxes(labels=…)` to query by tenant/role. Python + CLI.
- [12-interactive-shell](sandboxes/guides/12-interactive-shell/README.md):
  `aca sandbox shell` — interactive PTY session into a running sandbox. CLI only.
- [13-cli-reference](sandboxes/guides/13-cli-reference/README.md):
  `aca` CLI reference — installation, auth, help, global flags & short forms,
  env vars, config (`~/.aca/config.json`), `doctor`, YAML spec workflow,
  selectors, output formats, verbose/debug.
- [14-sdk-reference](sandboxes/guides/14-sdk-reference/README.md):
  Python SDK reference — installation, the three clients, `aio` namespace,
  logging, exceptions, helpers (`wait_for_running` / `ensure_running`,
  region helpers, constants), pollers (sync vs `begin_*`, polling kwargs,
  parallel fan-out).

## Sandboxes — scenarios (composed use cases)

- [01-webapps](sandboxes/scenarios/01-webapps/README.md):
  Web apps in sandboxes. Patterns: `simple-anonymous` (port open to the
  internet, ready) and `authenticated` (Entra-gated, planned).
- [02-coding-agents](sandboxes/scenarios/02-coding-agents/README.md):
  Run **GitHub Copilot CLI** inside a sandbox with deny-default egress
  and PAT-injection Transform rules (Python + CLI). The customer's PAT
  is attached to outbound requests by the egress proxy and never
  enters the sandbox. Claude Code / Codex stubs included.
- [03-code-interpreter](sandboxes/scenarios/03-code-interpreter/README.md):
  LLM-driven Python execution. Azure OpenAI drives a multi-turn
  `chat.completions` + tool-calling loop where each `python_exec` runs
  inside a fresh ACA microVM (Python disk + pandas/matplotlib). Includes
  a sample sales CSV, automatic artifact (plot) retrieval to the host's
  `./out/`, and explicit "deny-default egress" guidance. Same shape as
  OpenAI Code Interpreter, but the data and the sandbox stay in your
  tenant. `openai/` variant ships; `anthropic/` planned.
- [04-swarms](sandboxes/scenarios/04-swarms/README.md):
  Orchestrator coordinating many sandbox workers. Variant
  [`01-sandbox-inception`](sandboxes/scenarios/04-swarms/01-sandbox-inception/README.md)
  ships (orchestrator sandbox spawns N workers in another group via its
  group's system-assigned MI; Monte Carlo Pi demo across `asyncio.gather`
  in Python and `aca --managed-identity` + bash `&` in CLI). Variant
  [`02-shared-blob-memory`](sandboxes/scenarios/04-swarms/02-shared-blob-memory/README.md)
  ships (same inception shape plus a sandbox-group AzureBlob volume as
  durable shared scratchpad: workers stream JSON checkpoints, an
  aggregator sandbox lists the prefix after workers are gone).
- [05-data-processing](sandboxes/scenarios/05-data-processing/README.md):
  Producer/consumer ETL pipeline on a shared AzureBlob volume. A
  producer sandbox streams synthetic event batches into
  `/mnt/shared/raw/` while a transformer sandbox concurrently enriches
  them into `/mnt/shared/processed/`; an aggregator then summarises
  everything. All three workers are pure stdlib (`open()`, `glob`,
  `json`) — no Azure Blob SDK, no SAS tokens. Demonstrates
  stream-while-process, atomic writes via `os.replace`, `.done/`
  archive pattern, and zero blob plumbing. Composes guides 01 + 04.
- [06-developer-workflows](sandboxes/scenarios/06-developer-workflows/README.md):
  Ephemeral CI runner pattern — cold-boot a base sandbox, `pip install
  pytest`, snapshot the resulting "warm runner" image, then for each
  pending PR create a fresh sandbox from the snapshot, upload the PR
  diff with `write_file`, run `pytest`, and report pass/fail. Three
  synthetic PRs ship (2 pass, 1 with a deliberate `mul()` regression
  the tests catch) so first-time readers see both code paths. Exit
  code follows CI conventions (0 / 2 / 1). Composes guides 01
  (sandboxes) + 02 (snapshots) + 07 (files).
- [07-computer-use](sandboxes/scenarios/07-computer-use/README.md):
- [07-computer-use](sandboxes/scenarios/07-computer-use/README.md):
  LLM computer-use agent driving a Linux desktop inside a sandbox.
  Shared `desktop-image/` (Xvfb + Chrome + xdotool + noVNC + FastAPI
  control server + demo expense-report form). Ships the `openai/` flow:
  an Azure OpenAI `computer-use-preview` (gpt-5.4) deployment runs the
  Responses API loop via the OpenAI Agents SDK (`AsyncComputer` +
  `ComputerTool`), the `ACAAsyncComputer` adapter forwards each model
  action to the in-sandbox control server, the agent fills out the form,
  the script verifies `/tmp/submission.json`. noVNC URL printed so the
  operator can watch live; `--manual` flag skips the LLM for platform
  demos; `--prompt` + `--start-url` + `--allow-internet` aim it at any
  site. Egress = deny-by-default with no allow rules (target is
  sandbox-local). Composes guides 01 + 06 + 07 + 08; 03 for the optional
  disk-image speed-up.
- [08-sandbox-agents](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/README.md):
  Wire agent frameworks to an ACA sandbox as their tool-execution
  backend. Harness (model calls, planning) stays in your control
  plane; shell + files run inside a fresh sandbox per session.
  [`openai/`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/README.md)
  ships a **first-class sandbox provider** for the GA OpenAI Agents SDK
  ([`openai/extension/`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/extension/README.md))
  plus three runnable demos:
  ([`openai/sample/deep_research_agent.py`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/sample/deep_research_agent.py))
  single-agent research,
  ([`openai/sample/research_swarm.py`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/sample/research_swarm.py))
  parallel fan-out across 6 sandboxes, and
  ([`openai/sample/stateful_research_agent.py`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/sample/stateful_research_agent.py))
  cross-session memory via an Azure Blob volume (host-verified). All three
  research ACA Sandboxes using the repo's own
  [`guides/`](sandboxes/guides) as the corpus; the platform
  architecture brief lives at
  [`openai/PLATFORM.md`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/openai/PLATFORM.md).
  [`anthropic/`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/anthropic/README.md)
  (Claude Managed Agents self-hosted sandboxes) and
  [`langchain/`](sandboxes/scenarios/08-sandbox-agents/langchain/README.md)
  (Deep Agents `BaseSandbox`) are placeholders that will follow the
  same provider shape once their sandbox-provider surfaces stabilize.

- [10-connectors-triggers](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/README.md):
  Connector-gateway triggers push outside events (Office 365, SharePoint,
  OneDrive, …) into a sandbox webhook over HTTPS. Sub-scenarios:
  ([`setup/`](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/setup/README.md))
  provisions a connector gateway with SystemAssigned MI, an Office 365
  connection (one-time OAuth consent), and the gateway → connection
  access policy. Also ships an `azd up` flow at the scenario root
  ([`azure.yaml`](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/azure.yaml))
  — a tiny `infra/main.bicep` creates only the resource group; a
  `postprovision` hook delegates to the same imperative setup scripts
  (Bicep types for the preview connector-gateway APIs aren't published
  yet);
  ([`trigger-lifecycle/`](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/trigger-lifecycle/README.md))
  CRUD walk-through against a tiny in-script HTTP listener (no real
  mailbox needed) — discover ops, PUT / list / disable / enable / delete
  a trigger config, cleanup in trigger → port → sandbox order;
  ([`email-to-sandbox/`](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/email-to-sandbox/README.md))
  round-trip Office 365 inbox → trigger → stdlib Python listener in a
  sandbox → minimal stdlib processing (word/line/char counts) →
  acknowledgment reply back through the **same** connection
  (SendMailV2). Fires on emails whose
  subject contains `Feedback`. Outbound auth is injected by an egress
  transform rule that pulls a Bearer token from the sandbox-group MI.
  Both sub-scenarios ship Python and bash flavors. Uses
  `az rest` against `Microsoft.Web/connectorGateways` api-version
  `2026-05-01-preview` (no Python SDK yet); the gateway MI is added to
  the sandbox port's `entraId.objectIds` so the webhook POST is
  managed-identity authenticated.

## Apps

Coming in Phase 4. See [containerapps/README.md](containerapps/README.md).

## Connectors

Coming in Phase 6. See [connectors/README.md](connectors/README.md).

## Triggers

Coming as a separate pillar in Phase 7. See [triggers/README.md](triggers/README.md).
Today, connector-gateway triggers (webhook-style: Office 365, SharePoint,
OneDrive, …) ship as
[scenario 10 under sandboxes](sandboxes/scenarios/10-connectors-triggers/README.md).

## AI Gateway

Coming soon. See [ai-gateway/README.md](ai-gateway/README.md).

## AI app workloads

Coming in Phase 8. See [ai-apps/README.md](ai-apps/README.md).

## Reference

- [Azure Container Apps Python SDK (Early Access)](https://github.com/microsoft/azure-container-apps/blob/main/docs/early/python-sdk/README.md)
- [ACA CLI (Early Access)](https://github.com/microsoft/azure-container-apps/blob/main/docs/early/aca-cli/README.md)
